items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at East Middlefield Road
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential project at 490 East Middlefield Road on second reading at the March 24 meeting. This represents a new housing development in the city that will add residential units to the area.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city submitted an application for California's Prohousing Incentive Program Round 4 at the March 24 meeting. This state grant could bring funding to support housing development and reduce local barriers to new residential construction.

items.headline: City seeking public input on $HUD housing grant spending plan
items.summary: Santa Clara is accepting community feedback on how to spend federal HUD housing grants for the upcoming fiscal year. This is your chance to weigh in on housing priorities before the plan is finalized.

items.headline: City holds community meeting on safe parking program
items.summary: Sunnyvale held a public meeting on March 23 to discuss a safe parking program, which typically addresses where homeless individuals and vehicle dwellers can safely park overnight. This signals the city is actively tackling homelessness and vehicle residency issues.

items.headline: City authorizes Red Cross to use facilities during disasters
items.summary: Palo Alto approved a facility agreement allowing the American Red Cross to use designated city buildings as emergency shelters and disaster response centers. This formalizes how the city will coordinate with Red Cross during emergencies like fires or earthquakes.
